statement: Do not turn copilot-instructions.md into a mutable feature-status ledger or roadmap. Volatile implementation state belongs in human-facing docs or knowledge notes, while always-on instructions should stay stable, factual, and runtime-relevant.
publicSummary: Always-on instruction files lose effectiveness when they mix stable operating rules with fast-changing product status.
cautionNotes: This does not forbid product philosophy in always-on files; it forbids volatile rollout or target-state status that drifts quickly.
nonGeneralizationNotes: Do not publish private roadmap items or internal feature state while extracting this conclusion.
